reasoning: GEOMETRY CHECK: (1) Macro-feature count at 1x: the tile (visible as center preview in composite) shows approximately 4-6 distinct regions — green grass patches, yellow-tan earth, brown dirt patches, and possibly small stone chips. Count is within the 3-8 acceptable range. (2) Scale of largest feature: color/texture patches span roughly 10-14 pixels in the 32x32 tile, which is at the upper end of the 4-8px target; not catastrophic but borderline large. (3) vs 32x32 player sprite: the tile is fairly chromatic (green, yellow, brown all at moderate saturation), giving it energy comparable to a sprite — it does not clearly sit 'quieter' beneath objects. Overall the tile avoids total flatness and avoids noise-mush chaos, but the patch features are slightly oversized and the color contrast is energetic enough to compete with sprites placed on top. Marginal pass; would benefit from desaturating or reducing patch contrast.
